design_philosophy.core_loop: Place bombs to destroy walls and enemies, navigate grid-based maze, chain reactions via bomb-to-bomb detonation, collect power-ups to modify tactics
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Spatial puzzle solving under time pressure; balancing bomb placement (traps enemy or self) with safe escape routes; managing limited bomb slots and flame reach
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Tactical demolition expert controlling chain explosions to outwit opponents or clear obstacles; competitive bomb-based combat
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Grid-based movement restriction creates deliberate, non-twitch gameplay; bomb physics as core mechanic (chain reactions, early detonation via flame contact) rather than supplementary tool; emphasis on arena strategy over reflexes; multiplayer battle mode positions combat as core draw, not afterthought

difficulty.lives_system: Single-player Normal Game uses lives system; losses of non-permanent power-ups (firepower, bombs, speed retained across deaths)
difficulty.continues_system: Password system allows mid-game save/load at any cleared stage, eliminating traditional continue structure
difficulty.checkpointing: Password-based checkpointing at stage completion; no in-stage checkpoints documented
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Battle Mode trophy count (1-5) configurable; Speed Round (Stage 12) noted as highest skill requirement; two-minute round timer with wall encroachment after 90 seconds creates escalating pressure

progression.upgrade_system: Power-up items modify mechanics mid-stage (firepower range, bomb count, movement speed, invincibility); effects persist across lives in Normal Game but reset between Battle rounds
progression.unlock_mechanism: Six themed worlds with eight stages each; World 5 deviates with robot arena combat (no destructible walls, continuous enemy waves); boss fights cap each world
replayability_hooks: Battle Mode with 1-4 players on twelve themed stages
Speed Round competitive variant requiring high skill
Cooperative vs. competitive two-player Normal Game (friendly fire enabled)
Password system enables direct stage selection for speedrunning/challenge runs
Configurable trophy count (1-5) for tournament length variation

world_building_techniques: Thematic world design (six distinct environments implied by stage descriptions)
Plot framing: Diamond City conflict vs. Peace Town origin (Bomberman's home)
Character motivation: evil Carat Diamond and Dr. Mook orchestrate robot tournament to steal Bomberman's combat data

boss_design.philosophy: Stage-capping encounters; bosses appear at end of each world (6 worlds = 6 boss fights in Normal Game); robot Bombermen in World 5 arena
notable_bosses: World 5 robot Bombermen (arena-based, continuous waves, no destructible walls)

faction_indicators: Diamond City authority (Carat Diamond, Dr. Mook) vs. Peace Town (Bomberman origin)
Robot Bombermen faction (artificially engineered combat units)
danger_signals: Capability theft (stealing Bomberman's combat data) as espionage/power consolidation
environmental_storytelling: Six thematically distinct worlds suggest regional/cultural variation
Robot arena (World 5) distinct from natural/civilian stages: stark contrast implies technological incursion
